quotes.text: God sent angels to gather [Lot] and his family out of Sodom, while the wicked were destroyed by a devouring fire.
quotes.speaker: Joseph Smith
quotes.attr: Joseph Smith, Letter to W.W. Phelps, from Hiram, Ohio, 31 July 1832; PWJS, 247

notes: This is a parallel with the feast of Abraham in Genesis 18

quotes.text: Unleavened bread. This was something that could be baked in a few minutes. It is from here, however, that there is a tradition that this episode occurred on Passover (Rashi. See note on Genesis 17:23).
quotes.speaker: Navigating the Bible

notes: According to the Joseph Smith Translation, Lot did not offer to give the wicked men of the city his daughters, but instead said, "I … plead with my brethren that I may not bring them out unto you; and ye shall not do unto them as seemeth good in your eyes" (JST Genesis 19:13).

quotes.text: The Joseph Smith Translation states that it was "the angels of God, which were holy men," who, "pulled Lot into the house

notes: blindness (canver) blindness that comes on suddenly - a curse from God (see 2 Kings 6:18).
look (nabat) show regard to not behind thee ('achar) afterwards (of time) "Do not look behind" as an idiom means "Do not regret," "forget the past". (George Lamsa, Old Testament Light).
escape (malat) be delivered [notice they are fleeing to the mountain for protection from the world]

quotes.text: Zoar - Its original name was Bela (Genesis 14:2). It was south of the present Dead Sea (see note on Genesis 13:10).
quotes.speaker: Navigating the Bible

notes: brimstone (gophriyth) figurative of judgment from the root (gopher) the same word that describes the building material for Noah's ark. The judgment was on the outside and protection on the inside.

quotes.text: God is our Father. He is concerned about the welfare of His children everywhere. But even a patient, loving Heavenly Father must weary of tolerating ungodliness as evidenced when “the Lord rained upon Sodom and … Gomorrah brimstone and fire from … out of heaven” (Genesis 19:24).
quotes.speaker: David B. Haight
quotes.attr: David B. Haight, in Conference Report, Apr. 1990, 28; or Ensign, May 1990, 24

quotes.text: In consequence of rejecting the gospel of Jesus Christ and the prophets whom God has sent, the judgments of God have rested upon people, cities, and nations in various ages of the world, which was the case with the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah, who were destroyed for rejecting the prophets.
quotes.speaker: Joseph Smith's Commentary on the Bible
quotes.attr: Joseph Smith's Commentary on the Bible

notes: "She became a pillar of salt" is an Eastern idiom which means, "She had a stroke, or was paralyzed and dead." (George Lamsa, Old Testament Light).
looked back (nabat) show regard to, pay attention to, "turned back"

quotes.text: Josephus states that in his time, this pillar could still be seen (Antiquities 1:11:4). It was also known in Talmudic times (Berakhoth 54b). Significantly, at the southern end of the Dead Sea, there is a mountain of salt called Jebel Usdum (Sodom Mountain), 6 miles long, 3 miles wide, and some 1000 feet thick. Although it is now covered with a layer of earth several feet thick, the rest of the mountain is solid salt. This also supports the contention that Lot and his family were heading south toward Tzoar. A rain of salt was part of the upheaval (see Ibn Ezra here and Deuteronomy 29:22).
quotes.speaker: Navigating the Bible

notes: cave (me`arah) from (`uwr) to be exposed, be bared implying away from light
The Joseph Smith Translation explains that Lot's daughters "did wickedly and made their father drink wine . . . and lay with him"

quotes.text: The Moabites and Ammonites are descendants of Lot, Abraham’s nephew. When Sodom and Gomorrah were destroyed, Lot’s two daughters, believing that the world had come to an end, intoxicated their father and deceived him into fathering children by them. The Ammonites lived east of Mount Gilead, from the Jabbok southwards. They were subject to King David and although they later became independent, they nevertheless presented gifts to King Uzziah. Their brethren, the Moabites, lived along the eastern bank of the Jordan River and east of the Dead Sea. They warred constantly with Israel. Their language, known from the Moabite Stela, erected by King Mesha in honor of his victories over Israel, was almost identical to Hebrew.
quotes.speaker: John Tvedtnes
quotes.attr: John Tvedtnes, "Who Is an Arab?" Ensign, Apr. 1974, 28
